Refiled, Revised And Correct Petition To Contest Living Trust Of Gerald Deryan Probate Code 17200
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To Jun-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew DeRyan, petitioner in pro per.
Stephen McDonagh specially appeared for trustee Thomas Russell (via Zoom).
Frank Adam specially appeared for Marc Seidenfeld and Aishlin O'Coyle (via Zoom).
Andrew Schultz specially appeared for Witherspoon & Siracusa LLP (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on "Refiled, Revised and Correct Petition to Contest Living Trust of Gerald DeRyan."

At the outset, the Court disclosed prior familiarity with the decedent. No objections were raised.

The Court stated that it could not reach the merits of the petition at this time due to defects in notice and service. The Court noted that the revised petition appeared to allege claims sufficient to proceed, but that proper service must be completed and proofs of service filed before the matter could move forward. Counsel specially appearing confirmed that their clients had not been properly served.

All specially appearing counsel agreed to accept service by mail. The Court directed petitioner to serve the petition by mail with notice of the continued hearing date and stated that any properly served party should file a verified response. The Court indicated that it preferred the matter proceed on the merits rather than be resolved on procedural deficiencies, but that proper notice requirements must be satisfied.

The matter was continued to June 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Reported with FTR (D204)

Refiled, Revised And Correct Petition To Contest Living Trust Of Gerald Deryan Probate Code 17200
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Continued To Feb-09-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew DeRyan, petitioner in pro per.
Stephen McDonagh specially appeared on behalf of trustee Thomas Russell (via Zoom).
Frank Adam specially appeared on behalf of Marc Seidenfeld and Aishlin O'Coyle.

The matter was called for hearing on "Refiled, Revised, and Correct Petition to Contest Living Trust of Gerald DeRyan."

Due to communication and technical difficulties with the petitioner's telephonic appearance, the Court stated it was not comfortable proceeding with the hearing. The Court continued the matter to February 9, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The Court made a disclosure for the record regarding a limited prior acquaintance with the decedent through shared community affiliations many years ago, and stated that the disclosure did not affect the Court's ability to preside over the matter.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To Set Forth Why The San Francisco Court Is The Proper Venue For Determination Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Aug-28-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Extended Time
Off Calendar Aug-27-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew C. Deryan, Petitioner and moving party in Pro Per.
Stephen McDonagh specially appeared on behalf of trustee Thomas Russell (via Zoom).
Frank Adam specially appeared on behalf of former attorney Marc Seidenfeld (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on Mr. Deryan's motion for extended time.

The Court noted its tentative ruling, which found the motion failed to state a legally cognizable claim and did not identify a specific deadline subject to extension. The Court nevertheless allowed Petitioner to be heard.

Mr. Deryan argued that he is disabled, recovering from surgery, and requires additional time to file evidence. He asserted that the clerk's office improperly rejected filings by mail, forcing him to travel from Washington to file in person. He also alleged conflicts of interest involving former attorney Marc Seidenfeld (who he claimed was a former judge and engaged in fraud, undue influence, and elder abuse concerning the trust), requested the Court's recusal, and claimed his ADA rights were violated.

The Court stated it had no connection with Mr. Seidenfeld, who was never a judge of this Court, and explained that the deficiencies in the pleadings included lack of clarity as to parties and relief sought. Mr. McDonagh and Mr. Adam both concurred with the Court's assessment and noted valid service had not been effected.

Mr. Deryan insisted that he had complied with the Court's prior instructions, filed proof of service, and sought more time to file additional evidence. He maintained that his ADA rights were being violated and that dismissal would force him to pursue remedies in federal court.

The Court found that Mr. Deryan's motion did not state a proper legal basis and that his petition remained deficient. The Court denied the motion for extended time.

The Court further ordered Mr. Deryan's petition dismissed, vacated all pending hearings, and indicated it would issue a written order after hearing.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Reported with FTR/"For the Record" (D204)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Adam appeared via zoom for Mark Seidenfeld.
Matthew Deryan appeared via zoom propria persona.

Mr. DeRyan stated to the court that his uncle lived in San Francisco and died in San Francisco, that he filed in San Francisco for that reason, that he's unable to afford an attorney due to his fixed income and disablilty, and that he also believes that Mr. Seidenfeld engaged in unlawful collusion.

The Court instructed the petitioner to file this matter in the proper County. The Court also ordered that this matter be taken off calendar and all future hearings in this matter.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
